hotspots.title: Nucleus
hotspots.description: The nucleus is the control center of the cell. It contains the genetic material (DNA) and directs all cell activities.

hotspots.title: Nucleolus
hotspots.description: The nucleolus is found inside the nucleus and is responsible for producing ribosomes.

hotspots.title: Nuclear Membrane
hotspots.description: The nuclear membrane surrounds the nucleus and controls what enters and leaves it.

hotspots.title: Cytoplasm
hotspots.description: The cytoplasm is the jelly-like substance where organelles are suspended and cellular reactions occur.

hotspots.title: Cell Membrane
hotspots.description: The cell membrane surrounds the cell and regulates the movement of substances in and out.

hotspots.title: Mitochondrion
hotspots.description: Mitochondria produce energy through cellular respiration and are often called the powerhouse of the cell.

hotspots.title: Golgi Apparatus
hotspots.description: The Golgi apparatus modifies, packages, and transports proteins and other substances.

hotspots.title: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum
hotspots.description: The rough endoplasmic reticulum is covered with ribosomes and helps produce proteins.

hotspots.title: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum
hotspots.description: The smooth endoplasmic reticulum synthesizes lipids and helps detoxify harmful substances.

hotspots.title: Vesicle
hotspots.description: Vesicles are small membrane-bound sacs used to transport materials within the cell.

hotspots.title: Lysosome
hotspots.description: Lysosomes contain enzymes that break down waste materials and worn-out cell parts.

hotspots.title: Ribosomes
hotspots.description: Ribosomes are tiny structures responsible for protein synthesis.
